code:
class Solution:
    def sumSubarrayMins(self, arr: list[int]) -> int:
        MOD = 10**9 + 7
        n = len(arr)
        
        # prev_smaller[i] will store the index of the nearest element to the left of i that is strictly smaller than arr[i].
        # If no such element exists, it defaults to -1.
        prev_smaller = [-1] * n
        
        # next_smaller_or_equal[i] will store the index of the nearest element to the right of i that is smaller than or equal to arr[i].
        # If no such element exists, it defaults to n (array length).
        next_smaller_or_equal = [n] * n
        
        stack = [] # Monotonic stack to store indices
        
        # Calculate prev_smaller array
        # We iterate from left to right. When processing arr[i],
        # we pop elements from the stack that are greater than or equal to arr[i].
        # The top of the stack (if not empty) after popping will be prev_smaller[i].
        for i in range(n):
            while stack and arr[stack[-1]] >= arr[i]:
                stack.pop()
            if stack:
                prev_smaller[i] = stack[-1]
            stack.append(i)
            
        stack = [] # Reset stack for the next calculation
        
        # Calculate next_smaller_or_equal array
        # We iterate from right to left. When processing arr[i],
        # we pop elements from the stack that are strictly greater than arr[i].
        # The top of the stack (if not empty) after popping will be next_smaller_or_equal[i].
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            while stack and arr[stack[-1]] > arr[i]: # Use strictly greater for next_smaller_or_equal to handle duplicates correctly
                stack.pop()
            if stack:
                next_smaller_or_equal[i] = stack[-1]
            stack.append(i)
            
        total_sum = 0
        
        # Calculate the contribution of each element arr[i]
        # For each arr[i], it is the minimum in (i - prev_smaller[i]) * (next_smaller_or_equal[i] - i) subarrays.
        # (i - prev_smaller[i]) is the count of valid left boundaries (from prev_smaller[i] + 1 to i).
        # (next_smaller_or_equal[i] - i) is the count of valid right boundaries (from i to next_smaller_or_equal[i] - 1).
        for i in range(n):
            left_count = i - prev_smaller[i]
            right_count = next_smaller_or_equal[i] - i
            
            # The contribution of arr[i] to the total sum
            total_sum = (total_sum + arr[i] * left_count * right_count) % MOD
            
        return total_sum
prompt: Sum of subarray minimums using stack yaz.
